findings.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
findings.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
findings.what: 322 of 322 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
findings.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

findings.effort: Usually a bulk edit in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Populate trip_headsign to match what the bus displays.
findings.why: Riders at the stop can't tell which direction a bus is going.

findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 0% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (0% marked accessible, 0% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'foreign_key_violation' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Foreign key violation (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'duplicate_key' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Duplicate key (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'invalid_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Invalid url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'invalid_row_length' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Invalid row length (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_diff_coordinates_distance_below_threshold' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Equal shape distance diff coordinates distance below threshold (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already expired.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.effort: Worth a vendor question if it appears often.
findings.fix: Check the flagged trips in your scheduling software; remove them or restore their missing stops.
findings.what: Some trips serve fewer than two stops.
findings.why: A trip with one stop can't be ridden; planners ignore it and it may signal an export problem.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unused_trip' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unused trip (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: Usually a re-export from your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Export and publish an updated GTFS feed that covers at least the next 30 days of service.
findings.what: This feed's service calendar runs out within the next 7 days.
findings.why: When the calendar ends, Google Maps and other trip planners drop your agency entirely. Riders see no service at all.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing feed contact email and url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Trim past service periods the next time you export.
findings.what: Many of the feed's trips don't run at all in the next 7 days.
findings.why: It usually means old service periods are still in the export, making the feed bigger and harder to check.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unsorted_stop_times' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unsorted stop times (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 12 kinds of issue across 47 instances (9 error, 36 warning, 2 informational).

findings.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
findings.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data ended 916 day(s) ago. Trip planners have likely already dropped this feed.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Not scored yet. Nothing here counts against the grade.

criteria.detail: States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 100% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. 4 validator errors to resolve. Service data expired 914 days ago. States wheelchair access on 0% of stops and 100% of trips; the mark needs 90% of each.

feed.license_note: No stated data license in the Mobility Database; verify before publishing.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Setting your GTFS agency_id to your five-digit NTD ID is an optional way to line a feed up with its National Transit Database record. FTA links the two on your P-50 form, so it is not a required feed change. We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data expired 914 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

ntd_readiness.summary: Resolve this before you certify on the D-10. 4 validator errors to resolve. Service data expired 914 days ago, so FTA would find the link out of date.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 295 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("Central Park Blvd. @ Funland", "Fall Hill Ave. @ Forest Village Apts.", "Train Station (Caroline St.)", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

findings.effort: Usually an export setting or a stop_times mapping in your software.
findings.fix: Check your scheduling export: every trip should list each stop it calls at, in order, with times.
findings.what: 2 of 171 trips list fewer than two stops.
findings.why: A trip with one stop has no leg a rider can ride; trip planners can't route anyone on it, so the service effectively does not exist.

findings.effort: A cleanup pass in your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Remove stops no route serves, or add the trips that should call at them.
findings.what: 5 of 322 boardable stops are never served by any trip.
findings.why: Riders see these stops in apps and on the map but can never catch anything there, which erodes trust in the data.

top_fixes.effort: Usually one export setting; export on a schedule so it never lapses again.
top_fixes.fix: Re-export the feed with a calendar that reaches further out, and set feed_info feed_end_date past your next service change.
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency even though the buses are still running. Riders are told the service does not exist.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'foreign_key_violation' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Foreign key violation (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

top_fixes.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'duplicate_key' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
top_fixes.what: Duplicate key (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
top_fixes.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.
